# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
  instance_group:
    type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
    properties:
      name: quick-instance-group
      zone: us-central1-a
      instances:
        - { name: quick-instance-0, machineType: f1-micro }

  default-priority-scheduling-policy:
    type: compute.v1.autoscalersetting
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      autoscalingPolicy:
        maxReplicas: 5
        minReplicas: 3

  targetPool:
    type: compute.v1.targetpool
    properties:
      name: quick-target-pool
      zone: us-central1-a

  instance-template:
    type: compute.v1.instanceTemplate
    properties:
      name: quick-instance-template
      machineType: f1-micro
      properties:
        canIpForward: true
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          mode: READ_WRITE
          initializeParams:
            diskSizeGb: 10
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/debian-9-stretch-v20190904
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: { project: 'your-project-id', name: 'default' }
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

  instance-group-managed:
    type: compute.v1.instanceGroupManager
    properties:
      baseInstanceName: quick-instance
      targetPoolManagedInstances: [ { instance: '{ selfLink: "resource(quick-instance-template).selfLink" }' } ]
